notes: all combat numbers are simulator output on unverified constants
checkpoint levels are provisional (no level timeline in the match feed)
evidence deltas carry finished-inventory survivorship bias

coachLine: Sim-optimal for sustained DPS: first spike lands around minute 12 (Viper), third item by minute 25. Take Demiurge: +16.2% on this kit’s main output at minute 15.

all.name: Marrow
all.note: Marrow (Major): missing-health-dependent omnivamp; no sustain-uptime model yet

all.name: Nihil
all.note: Nihil (Major): accrual rate depends on combat uptime (how long Doom persists per fight), which the closed-form sim cannot observe

all.name: Aion
all.note: Aion (Major): economy effect; valuable but outside combat objectives (revisit with gold curves)

all.name: Exarch
all.note: Exarch (Major): buffs the HEAL TARGET (usually an ally) — team-side value outside the single-hero sim; self-heal uptime depends on heal cadence vs the 4s window

augments.name: Spray 'N Pray
augments.note: accuracy penalty at long range is positional, not scored - treat as close-range value

metaBuilds.whyLine: Strongest meta core for sustained auto DPS, online around minute 25 — about 308 auto-attack DPS in the sim.
metaBuilds.optimizer: Optimizer sees +47% more sustained auto DPS: try Viper over Solaris (sim-only — the popular pick may carry utility the sim can't see; test it).

metaBuilds.whyLine: Strongest meta core for one-combo burst, online around minute 26 — about 674 damage in one combo in the sim.
Strongest meta core for extended fights vs bruisers, online around minute 25 — about 558 damage over a 20s fight in the sim.

matchups.gameplan: Your kill threat peaks early-to-mid: cash it in before their later items level the exchange.

counterSwap.line: Losing lane? Third item Warden's Faith instead of Plasma Blade: their 3s all-in loses 36% of its bite; you give up 35% of your damage.

matchups.gameplan: They out-threaten you whenever the lane is not even: treat this as a farm-and-team lane, not a duel.

counterSwap.line: Losing lane? Third item Raiment of Renewal instead of Plasma Blade: their 3s all-in loses 24% of its bite; you give up 35% of your damage.

matchups.gameplan: They out-threaten you whenever the lane is not even: treat this as a farm-and-team lane, not a duel.

counterSwap.line: Losing lane? Third item Raiment of Renewal instead of Plasma Blade: their 3s all-in loses 24% of its bite; you give up 35% of your damage.

matchups.gameplan: Your window is early: force trades before their core completes, and do not coin-flip fights past the crossover.

counterSwap.line: Losing lane? Third item Raiment of Renewal instead of Plasma Blade: their 3s all-in loses 24% of its bite; you give up 35% of your damage.

matchups.gameplan: Respect their early kill threat; the exchange levels out later, so trade patiently and scale.

counterSwap.line: Losing lane? Third item Warden's Faith instead of Plasma Blade: their 3s all-in loses 36% of its bite; you give up 35% of your damage.

matchups.gameplan: Your window is early: force trades before their core completes, and do not coin-flip fights past the crossover.

counterSwap.line: Losing lane? Third item Raiment of Renewal instead of Plasma Blade: their 3s all-in loses 24% of its bite; you give up 35% of your damage.

laneSteer.provenance: “Amplified Burst” ⇒ poke; the sim can’t model this augment — steered by the declared playstyle + field evidence — field carry 47.5% over 408,622 games, magnitude not simulated.
